title: The Deep-Patience Station

features.name: waiting-shed
features.desc-on-touch: a small shelter built into the rock, its walls worn smooth by decades of floodwater. people waited here for the river to pass. now the river waits with them

features.name: stone-speech
features.desc-on-touch: the river has carved words into the bedrock. you cannot read them, but you can feel the patience in their grooves—each one took a decade to deepen

features.name: biscuit-line
features.desc-on-touch: a shelf in the shed still holds the phantom of biscuits eaten long ago. the archive smells them, though there is nothing there. the river taught patience; the river also recalls

features.name: deep-current
features.desc-on-touch: the water moves here with a pressure you can feel in your bones. it's not violent; it's patient. it's been teaching the same lesson for ten thousand years

features.name: memory-timber
features.desc-on-touch: old wooden beams still in the shed, each ring a year the river watched from this bend. you can read the flood years in the scars

inscription: here patience and pressure are the same thing, taught by stone and water for longer than anyone remembers

file.name: The_First_Aid_Keeper_of_the_Deep_Bend.txt
file.note: a memoir fragment of someone who tended this station. the archive cannot decide if it ends at their departure or continues beyond it

exits.label: back upriver to where the river teaches
across the stones to the eucalyptus where others have also waited
